summary: In this brief excerpt, Anne Wojcicki reflects on the 20th anniversary of the sequencing of the human genome. She argues that while scientific progress has been incredible, the healthcare system has failed to implement widespread genetic testing to actually deliver on the promise of personalized healthcare.

principles.statement: The barrier to personalized healthcare is implementation, not a lack of scientific progress.
principles.rationale: Despite sequencing the human genome 20 years ago, we are still far from delivering personalized healthcare because we haven't implemented widespread genetic testing.
principles.source_quote: The problem is not a lack of scientific progress, but rather a failure to implement widespread genetic testing

signature_quotes.text: The problem is not a lack of scientific progress, but rather a failure to implement widespread genetic testing
signature_quotes.context: Discussing why personalized healthcare is not yet a reality 20 years after the human genome was sequenced.

signature_quotes.text: Yet, we are still far from the delivery of personalized healthcare for everyone.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the unfulfilled promise of the human genome project.

anti_patterns.description: Failing to implement widespread genetic testing in standard patient journeys.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It prevents the realization of personalized healthcare, leaving the promise of the human genome project unfulfilled despite sufficient scientific progress.
